zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Skelton於1278年出生在英國時，他的父親Edward Skelton26歲，母親Mrs. Edward Skelton25歲。他於大約1306年與Sigreda de Skelton在英國波藍德阿馬維艾特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1342年1月在英國波藍德阿馬維艾特享年64歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Skelton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire): habitational name mainly from Skelton in Cumberland, or any of various places called Skelton in Yorkshire, North Yorkshire, and East Yorkshire. The placenames probably all derive from Old English scelf ‘rock, ledge, shelf’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, with initial Sk-, instead of Sh-, due to Scandinavian influence. However, in some cases, the first element may be a lost river name, from Old Norse skjallr ‘resounding’; compare Skelding . This English surname has been established in Queen's County [Laois], Ireland, since the 16th century.

variants.name: Skelding
meanings: English: habitational name from Skelding (Yorkshire). The place takes its name from the river Skell, itself from Old Norse skjallr ‘resounding’, + Old English denu ‘valley’. In addition, the name may come from Skeldon (Ayrshire), although there is a lack of evidence for a historic presence in this part of Scotland.

variants.name: Skilton
meanings: English (Surrey): probably a variant of Skelton , though in some cases the name may be a variant of Shilton, a habitational name from Shilton (Warwickshire, Oxfordshire) or Earl Shilton (Leicestershire), from Old English scelf, scylf ‘rock, ledge, shelf’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Skelton was born in 1278, in England, his father, Edward Skelton, was 26 and his mother, Mrs. Edward Skelton, was 25. He married Sigreda de Skelton about 1306, in Armathwaite, Cumberland, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died in January 1342, in Armathwaite, Cumberland, England, at the age of 64.

ko.lifeSummary: John de Skelton(이)가 1278년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Skelton(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Edward Skelton(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1306년 영국 c벌랜드 아마스웨이트에서 Sigreda de Skelton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1342년 1월 영국 c벌랜드 아마스웨이트에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.
